sentence.en: There are bandits in the mountains.

匾.origin: 匾 is a flat board or tablet, often hung as a sign.

sentence.en: A plaque hangs above the door.

卅.origin: 卅 means thirty; it looks like three tally marks joined together.

sentence.en: He is already thirty years old.

卒.origin: 卒 means a soldier, a foot soldier in armor.

博.origin: 博 means wide or abundant in knowledge.

卜.origin: 卜 means divination; it looks like a crack on an oracle bone.

sentence.en: Ancient people divined with turtle shells.

占.origin: 占 means to occupy or to take up space.

卡.origin: 卡 means a card; picture it stuck between up 上 and down 下.

卢.origin: 卢 is a common Chinese family name.

卤.origin: 卤 means brine; it can mean to stew food in salty sauce.

卦.origin: 卦 names the symbols used in fortune-telling.

卧.origin: 卧 means to lie down or recline.

卫.origin: 卫 means to guard or protect.

卯.origin: 卯 is the fourth Earthly Branch, used in old timekeeping.

word.en: the hour of the rabbit (5-7 am)

印.origin: 印 means to print or a stamp, like a hand pressing a seal.

危.origin: 危 means dangerous, like a person at the edge of a cliff.

sentence.en: Watch out for danger when crossing the road.

即.origin: 即 means at once or right away.

卵.origin: 卵 means an egg; it pictures two ovaries holding eggs.

sentence.en: The bird lays eggs in the nest.

卷.origin: 卷 means to roll up, like a curled scroll.

卸.origin: 卸 means to unload or take off.

卿.origin: 卿 means a high official or noble in old times.

sentence.en: He is a high official at court.

厂.origin: 厂 means a factory; it also pictures a cliff overhang.

历.origin: 历 means to experience or a calendar.

压.origin: 压 means to press down, like weight crushing soil.

sentence.en: The stone presses down the paper.

厌.origin: 厌 means to dislike or be tired of something.

厘.origin: 厘 means one hundredth, a tiny unit of measure.

sentence.en: This line is five centimeters long.

原.origin: 原 means the source or original.

sentence.en: Oh, it turns out to be you.

县.origin: 县 means a county, a small district of land.

sentence.en: My home is in a small county town.

叁.origin: 叁 is the formal form of three, used on checks to prevent fraud.

sentence.en: The check is written for three yuan.

叉.origin: 叉 means a fork, like a hand grasping with crossed fingers.

sentence.en: I eat noodles with a fork.

双.origin: 双 means a pair, two hands 又 side by side.

sentence.en: I bought a pair of shoes.

叔.origin: 叔 means uncle, a father's younger brother.

sentence.en: Uncle comes to my home to play.

叙.origin: 叙 means to tell or narrate.

叠.origin: 叠 means to fold or stack up.

sentence.en: I fold my quilt every day.

叩.origin: 叩 means to knock or to kowtow.

sentence.en: He knocks softly on the door.

召.origin: 召 means to summon or call together.

叭.origin: 叭 imitates the sound of a horn or trumpet.

叮.origin: 叮 means to urge, or the sound of a small bell or sting.

sentence.en: Mom urges me to be careful.

台.origin: 台 means a platform or stage.

史.origin: 史 means history or record, like a hand writing it down.

sentence.en: This is a piece of history.

叹.origin: 叹 means to sigh, breath leaving the mouth 口.

叼.origin: 叼 means to hold something in the mouth.

sentence.en: The puppy holds a bone in its mouth.

吁.origin: 吁 is an exclamation, like a sigh of alas.

sentence.en: Everyone calls for protecting the environment.

吊.origin: 吊 means to hang something up.

sentence.en: A lamp hangs from the ceiling.

同.origin: 同 means same or together, sharing one voice 口.

吐.origin: 吐 means to spit out or vomit from the mouth 口.
